summary: The repair candidate preserves the underlying D+Q bone RCT safety limitation: the original extraction-refresh candidate remains in needs_revision and explicitly states that event-specific adverse-event extraction must be repaired before safety synthesis.

findings.claim_or_issue: The proposed update to senolytics-bone-rct-extraction-refresh-2026-06-21 keeps lifecycle_status as needs_revision and states that safety-limitations findings remain open until event-specific adverse-event extraction repairs are completed.
findings.why_it_matters: The supervisor lane would be unsafe if the repair hid or downgraded the open event-specific adverse-event gap from the original safety-limitations review.
findings.recommended_action: Accept this review lane while keeping the underlying event-specific adverse-event extraction work open for a later evidence repair.

findings.claim_or_issue: The revision-state repair candidate only tracks the repair candidate and updates candidate lifecycle/summary state; it does not promote the D+Q bone RCT candidate or claim complete safety synthesis.
findings.why_it_matters: Safety limitations remain bounded to candidate readiness and are not converted into an accepted evidence synthesis claim.
findings.recommended_action: No additional safety-limitations repair is required for this supervisor-review lane.
